title: Prepare for timing pulley replacement and access the motor area
summary: Safely prepare for timing pulley replacement by applying lockout/tagout to the tipper, gathering the specified replacement part, PPE, and tools, avoiding use of the round end of the hex wrench, and removing the station side panel if needed to access the motor. Figure 7-20 is used to identify the timing pulley assembly components and the referenced 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when preparing for timing pulley replacement and when access to the motor area is required as part of the timing pulley removal activity described in section 7.3.19 / 7.3.19.1 of the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a complete timing pulley removal or installation procedure; the packet only contains the initial preparation and access steps.
Do not use the round end of the hex wrench.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, gather the specified tools and PPE, remove the station side panel if needed, and identify the timing pulley assembly components using Figure 7-20.

safety_notes: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2.
Do not use the round end of the hex wrench.
Stop if lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.
Stop if the side panel cannot be safely removed or the motor area cannot be accessed with the specified fasteners and tools.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the tipper and station side panel

access_required: Access to the station side panel
Access to the Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure
system_preconditions: Timing pulley replacement preparation is required.
The tipper must be locked out before access work proceeds.
external_references: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure (referenced as page 2 in the source).

visual_references.description: Figure 7-20 timing pulley maintenance diagram showing set screws, 1108 taper-lock bushing, timing pulley, gearbox pilot face, and 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap.

steps.title: Gather replacement part, PPE, and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure all required items are available before beginning timing pulley replacement access work.
steps.instruction: Gather the listed replacement part, PPE, and tools: pulley, safety glasses, gloves, torque wrench, 1/8-in. hex wrench, 8-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench or socket.
steps.expected_result: All listed parts, PPE, and tools are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: The replacement pulley, PPE, and each listed tool are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: One or more required items are missing or unavailable.

actions.label: Verify required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the listed replacement part, PPE, and tools: pulley, safety glasses, gloves, torque wrench, 1/8-in. hex wrench, 8-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench or socket.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the timing pulley figure as the visual reference for the assembly you are preparing to access.

stop_or_escalate_if: Required replacement part, PPE, or tools are not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby procedure text lists required tools including a torque wrench, 1/8-in. hex wrench, 8-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench or socket, with PPE and replacement pulley for timing pulley replacement.

steps.title: Avoid using the round end of the hex wrench
steps.purpose: Prevent improper tool use during timing pulley service preparation.
steps.instruction: Do not use the round end of the hex wrench.
steps.expected_result: Only the correct end of the hex wrench is used during the procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The technician avoids the round end of the hex wrench.
steps.failure_condition: The round end of the hex wrench is used or is about to be used.

actions.label: Confirm correct hex wrench end
actions.exact_action: Do not use the round end of the hex wrench.

stop_or_escalate_if: There is uncertainty about which end of the hex wrench is acceptable to use.
The round end of the hex wrench would be required to continue.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby procedure text notes important service context such as not using the round end of the hex wrench.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before accessing the motor area.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out and tagged out in accordance with the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO is completed and the tipper is in a safe maintenance state.
steps.failure_condition: LOTO cannot be completed or the equipment cannot be placed in the required safe state.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2.

stop_or_escalate_if: Lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.
The tipper cannot be confirmed in a safe maintenance state.

steps.title: Remove the side panel fasteners if motor access is needed
steps.purpose: Open the station side panel mounting points so the panel can be removed for motor access.
steps.instruction: If necessary to access the motor, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.
steps.expected_result: The nine self-tapping fasteners are removed when side panel access is required.
steps.healthy_condition: All required side panel fasteners are removed without preventing safe panel removal.
steps.failure_condition: The fasteners cannot be removed safely or the panel remains secured.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the timing pulley assembly figure as the maintenance visual for the motor-area access context during timing pulley replacement.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel fasteners cannot be safely removed.
The specified 3/8-in. wrench or socket does not allow safe removal.
Motor access still cannot be obtained after fastener removal.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby procedure text notes the initial removal steps to LOTO the tipper and remove the station side panel if needed to access the motor.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel
steps.purpose: Gain physical access to the motor area for the timing pulley replacement work.
steps.instruction: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed and the motor area is accessible.
steps.healthy_condition: The side panel is removed and the motor area can be reached for the next maintenance steps.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed safely or the motor area remains inaccessible.

actions.label: Remove station side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference the timing pulley assembly diagram while opening access to the motor area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel cannot be safely removed.
The motor area cannot be accessed after panel removal.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-20 is the maintenance diagram associated with timing pulley replacement and motor-area access context.

steps.title: Identify timing pulley assembly components in Figure 7-20
steps.purpose: Confirm the technician can recognize the timing pulley assembly features referenced by the source before continuing with replacement work.
steps.instruction: Identify the timing pulley components shown in Figure 7-20, including the set screws, 1108 taper-lock bushing, timing pulley, gearbox pilot face, and the noted 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap.
steps.expected_result: The timing pulley assembly components and referenced gap are correctly identified from the source figure.
steps.healthy_condition: The technician can locate the set screws, 1108 taper-lock bushing, timing pulley, gearbox pilot face, and 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap in Figure 7-20.
steps.failure_condition: The required components or the referenced gap cannot be confidently identified from the figure.

actions.label: Review Figure 7-20 labels
actions.exact_action: Identify the timing pulley components shown in Figure 7-20, including the set screws, 1108 taper-lock bushing, timing pulley, gearbox pilot face, and the noted 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Set screws, 1108 taper-lock bushing, timing pulley, gearbox pilot face, and the 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap.

stop_or_escalate_if: The timing pulley components shown in Figure 7-20 cannot be matched to the equipment being serviced.
The 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap reference cannot be identified from the source figure.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-20 appears on page 182 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_19_1_removal.
This maintenance diagram includes labeled references to set screws, the 1108 taper-lock bushing, the timing pulley, the gearbox pilot face, and an 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap.

success_criteria: The required replacement part, PPE, and tools are gathered.
The tipper is locked out and tagged out using the referenced procedure.
The station side panel is removed if needed for access.
The motor area is accessible for further timing pulley replacement work.
The timing pulley assembly components in Figure 7-20 are identified.
healthy_conditions: Required tools and PPE are available before work begins.
LOTO is completed before access work proceeds.
The side panel can be removed using the specified fasteners and tool.
Figure 7-20 clearly supports identification of the timing pulley assembly components and the 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap.
failure_conditions: Lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.
Required tools, PPE, or replacement part are missing.
The side panel cannot be safely removed.
The motor area cannot be accessed.
The round end of the hex wrench is used or would need to be used.
The timing pulley components or referenced gap cannot be identified from Figure 7-20.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Stop and escalate if the side panel cannot be safely removed or the motor area cannot be accessed with the specified fasteners and tools.
Escalate for SME review if additional timing pulley removal or installation steps are needed, because they are not present in this packet.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing pulley assembly labels and the 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap reference.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-20 appears on page 182 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_19_1_removal.

missing_details: The packet does not include the full timing pulley removal procedure.
The packet does not include installation or reassembly steps.
The packet does not provide an estimated time for this procedure.
The packet does not specify whether production stop is required beyond the need for LOTO.
The source section text is not included in the packet; final wording is grounded from the candidate and artifact retrieval text only.
